notes: Compressed proxy for a real long session: FOUR plain user prompts, EACH eliciting a file-edit tool call, in ONE long-lived aider REPL session. The spec asserts (a) WITHIN each turn state stays continuously working across the interleaved assistant fragments and the edit tool_result — no flicker; (b) BETWEEN turns a clean working→ready→working reset, four ready→working→ready arcs on the SAME session; (c) state never wedges and never enters waiting; (d) the final state is ready. WHY interactive (not headless `aider --message`): headless aider is single-shot and exits after one reply, so it can't show turn-to-turn state reset, and an early exit truncates the terminal working→ready settle before process_exited. The aider interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh) keeps ONE REPL alive across all four send+wait_turn cycles and kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE process_exited, not four. AIDER TURN-END IS SYNTHESIZED, NOT MARKED: the markdown transcript .aider.chat.history.md has NO turn-end marker — a `> Tokens:` line closes one model CALL (assistant_message), and `> Applied edit to <file>` is a collapsed tool_result; neither is terminal in the state classifier (only turn_done with no open tool and no trailing question flips working→ready), which is exactly why the WITHIN-turn no-flicker invariant is ROBUST across the assistant fragments + edit tool_result. End-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms) quiet, surfaced behind the daemon's SessionDetector 5s working-refresh poll, so the EFFECTIVE working→ready latency after a turn's last line is ~5s. THE BETWEEN-TURN ARC IS THE FRAGILE PART AND IT COMPOUNDS: each of the four boundaries independently needs the next `####` prompt to NOT outrun the idle window; if any prompt lands before IdleFlush fires, the parser opens the new turn WITHOUT an intervening turn_done and the session stays working across that boundary, losing one per-turn reset (TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking). Over four turns the cumulative blur risk is higher than basic-turn, which is why the matrix marks this cell observes:partial and the recipe uses GENEROUS inter-turn sleeps (≥7s, one full ~5s effective latency plus slack) after every prompt so all four flips fire AND are observed before the next prompt re-enters working. TOOL CALLS (the scenario's contains_tool_call signal): each turn asks aider to make a small file edit, observable in transcript.jsonl as the collapsed aider tool_result the parser emits from the `> Applied edit to <file>` marker; the phase DSL has NO tool-event form, so the tool call is asserted only via the working span it sustains, not a dedicated phase, and its visibility is COARSE (one marker line per tool, no args/result payload — the observes:partial caveat). LAZY TRANSCRIPT: .aider.chat.history.md (FilesUnderCWD) materializes only once the first user input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) before the per-cwd transcript appears — session_birth, which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch', legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARCS, by contrast, must all land on the session that goes working: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (the pre-session never goes working); every later turn phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. PER-TURN TOKEN ACCUMULATION (the scenario's monotonic TotalTokens signal) isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); aider's cost_tracking:false means only sent/received token snapshots are carried from each `> Tokens:` line, no provider dollar cost — covered out-of-band by parser unit tests (TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn). HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, tool-result content, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); the working→ready edges land with ~5s effective idle-flush latency (a timing delay, not a correctness gap). Stable structurally: FOUR ready→working→ready arcs on ONE session, an applied edit inside each working span, a single process_exited, and a final ready state — never waiting. NOTE: this spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET — validated when aider auth is available in-env (no provider key present at authoring time; headless aider hangs ~5min on the OpenRouter OAuth prompt and the rig precheck delegates auth to the user).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the lazy transcript has not materialized yet)
First prompt (eliciting a file edit) → working (binds the live aider session; the pre-session never enters working)

First turn ends → back to ready (IdleFlush synthesizes turn-end after the prompt goes quiet); WITHIN the turn state stayed continuously working across the assistant fragments and the edit tool_result (no flicker — the only ready in this window is the turn end itself), and never entered waiting
Second prompt (eliciting a file edit) → working again on the same session
Second turn ends → back to ready; no mid-turn flicker, never entered waiting
Third prompt (eliciting a file edit) → working again on the same session
Third turn ends → back to ready; no mid-turn flicker, never entered waiting
Fourth prompt (eliciting a file edit) → working again on the same session
Final turn ends in ready; state stayed continuously working within the turn, the session never wedged in a single state across the four turns, and never entered waiting
PID bound to the live aider session
REPL torn down once after the fourth turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
